faqs.question: How do I know if I have drain flies in my Wakefield home?
faqs.answer: Drain flies are small, fuzzy-winged insects typically found near sinks, drains, showers, and bathrooms. You'll often see them resting on walls or ceilings near plumbing fixtures. They breed in the organic matter that builds up inside drains, so if you notice these small flies hovering around your bathroom or kitchen drains, you likely have an infestation that requires professional treatment.

faqs.question: What does your drain fly treatment process include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive treatment includes a thorough inspection to identify breeding sites, mechanical cleaning of affected drains to remove organic buildup, application of professional-grade bio-enzymatic treatments that break down larvae and eggs, and follow-up recommendations to prevent future infestations. We also inspect other potential breeding areas like air conditioning drains and floor drains.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take to work in Wakefield properties?
faqs.answer: Most customers see significant reduction in drain fly activity within 2-3 days after treatment. Complete elimination typically occurs within 7-10 days as the treatment breaks down breeding materials and eliminates larvae. Older Wakefield buildings with extensive plumbing may require follow-up treatments, which are included in our service guarantee.

faqs.question: Is drain fly treatment safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use eco-friendly, bio-enzymatic treatments that are safe for children and pets once applied. These solutions work by using natural bacteria and enzymes to break down organic matter in drains where drain flies breed. There are no harsh chemicals or fumigation required, and you can typically use your sinks and drains normally within a few hours of treatment.
